props.name: disabled
props.description:
Disables a button. Avoid disabling buttons because it will make them inaccessible to users
who rely on keyboard navigation. Buttons that are disabled cannot be clicked, selected, or navigated through.

props.name: size
props.description: Size of the button and fontSize of the text in the button

props.name: variant
props.description: Determines the styles on a button, one of 'default' | 'primary' | 'invisible' | 'danger' | 'link'

props.name: loading
props.description: Specify whether the button is in a loading state

props.name: block
props.description: Allow a button to fill its container horizontally

props.name: loadingAnnouncement
props.description: The content to announce to screen readers when loading

props.name: labelWrap
props.description: Whether the button label should wrap to multiple lines if it is longer than the button width

props.name: icon
props.description: Icon for the menu button

props.name: overflowIcon
props.description:
Icon displayed when the menu item is overflowing.
If 'none' is provided, no icon will be shown in the overflow menu.

props.name: returnFocusRef
props.description: Target element to return focus to when the menu is closed.

components.name: ActionList
components.description: A vertical list of interactive actions or options.

components.name: ActionMenu
components.description: this component is syntactical sugar 🍭

props.name: open
props.description: If defined, will control the open/closed state of the overlay. Must be used in conjunction with `onOpenChange`.

props.name: anchorRef
props.description:
An override to the internal ref that will be spread on to the renderAnchor
An override to the internal renderAnchor ref that will be used to position the overlay.
When renderAnchor is null this can be used to make an anchor that is detached from ActionMenu.

components.name: AnchoredOverlay
components.description: AnchoredOverlay opens an Overlay relative to the anchor position.

props.name: open
props.description: Determines whether the overlay portion of the component should be shown or not

props.name: overlayProps
props.type: Partial<Omit<DetailedHTMLProps<HTMLAttributes<HTMLDivElement>, HTMLDivElement>, "height" | "width" | "style" | ... 5 more ... | keyof ContainerProps> & Omit<...> & ContainerProps & { ...; }>
props.description: Props to be spread on the internal `Overlay` component.

props.name: focusTrapSettings
props.description: Settings to apply to the Focus Zone on the internal `Overlay` component.

props.name: focusZoneSettings
props.description: Settings to apply to the Focus Zone on the internal `Overlay` component.

props.name: className
props.description: Optional className to be added to the overlay component.

props.name: preventOverflow
props.description:
preventOverflow Optional. The Overlay width will be adjusted responsively if there is not enough space to display the Overlay.
If `preventOverflow` is `true`, the width of the `Overlay` will not be adjusted.

props.name: pinPosition
props.description: If true, the overlay will attempt to prevent position shifting when sitting at the top of the anchor.

props.name: variant
props.description: Optional prop to set variant for narrow screen sizes

props.name: displayCloseButton
props.description: Optional prop to display a close button in the overlay.

props.name: renderAnchor
props.description:
A custom function component used to render the anchor element.
Will receive the selected text as `children` prop when an item is activated.
A custom function component used to render the anchor element.
When renderAnchor is null, an anchorRef is required.

props.name: anchorRef
props.description:
An override to the internal ref that will be spread on to the renderAnchor
An override to the internal renderAnchor ref that will be used to position the overlay.
When renderAnchor is null this can be used to make an anchor that is detached from ActionMenu.

props.name: anchorId
props.description: An override to the internal id that will be spread on to the renderAnchor

components.name: BaseOverlay
components.description:
An `Overlay` is a flexible floating surface, used to display transient content such as menus,
selection options, dialogs, and more. Overlays use shadows to express elevation. The `Overlay`
component handles all behaviors needed by overlay UIs as well as the common styles that all overlays * should have.

props.name: border
props.description: Add a border around this component

components.name: BranchName
components.description: A label-type component that displays the name of a branch.

props.name: className
props.description: Optional class name for the breadcrumbs container.

props.name: overflow
props.description:
Controls the overflow behavior of the breadcrumbs.
By default all overflowing crumbs will "wrap" in the given space taking up extra height.
In the "menu" option we'll see the overflowing crumbs as part of a menu like dropdown instead of the root breadcrumb.
In "menu-with-root" we see that instead of the root, the menu button will take the place of the next breadcrumb.

props.name: variant
props.description:
Controls the visual variant of the breadcrumbs.
By default, the breadcrumbs will have a normal appearance.
In the "spacious" option, the breadcrumbs will have increased padding and a more relaxed layout.

props.name: style
props.description: Allows passing of CSS custom properties to the breadcrumbs container.

props.name: validationStatus
props.description: Only used to inform ARIA attributes. Individual checkboxes do not have validation styles.

props.name: indeterminate
props.description: Apply indeterminate visual appearance to the checkbox

props.name: className

props.name: disabled

props.name: id
props.description:
The unique identifier for this input group. Used to associate the label, validation text, and caption text.
You may want a custom ID to make it easier to select elements in integration tests.

props.name: required
props.description: If true, the user must make a selection before the owning form can be submitted

components.name: ConfirmationDialog

props.name: title
props.type: string | number | bigint | false | true | ReactElement<unknown, string | JSXElementConstructor<any>> | Iterable<ReactNode> | ReactPortal | Promise<AwaitedReactNode>
props.description:
Required. The title of the ConfirmationDialog. This is usually a brief
question.

props.name: cancelButtonContent
props.type: string | number | bigint | false | true | ReactElement<unknown, string | JSXElementConstructor<any>> | Iterable<ReactNode> | ReactPortal | Promise<AwaitedReactNode>
props.description: The text to use for the cancel button. Default: "Cancel".

props.name: confirmButtonContent
props.type: string | number | bigint | false | true | ReactElement<unknown, string | JSXElementConstructor<any>> | Iterable<ReactNode> | ReactPortal | Promise<AwaitedReactNode>
props.description: The text to use for the confirm button. Default: "OK".

props.name: confirmButtonType
props.description: The type of button to use for the confirm button. Default: Button.

props.name: cancelButtonLoading
props.description: Whether the cancel button is in a loading state. Default: false.

props.name: confirmButtonLoading
props.description: Whether the confirm button is in a loading state. Default: false.

props.name: overrideButtonFocus
props.description:
Overrides the button that should be initially focused when the dialog is opened. By default, the confirm button
is focused initially unless it is a dangerous action, in which case the cancel button is focused. This should
rarely be overridden, in order to ensure that the user does not accidentally confirm a dangerous action.

props.name: className
props.description: Additional class names to apply to the dialog

props.name: width
props.description:
The width of the dialog.
small: 296px
medium: 320px
large: 480px
xlarge: 640px

props.name: height
props.description:
The height of the dialog.
small: 480px
large: 640px
auto: variable based on contents

props.name: as
props.description: Provide an optional element type for the outermost element rendered by the component.

components.name: CounterLabel
components.description: Adds a count to navigational elements and buttons.

components.name: DataTable
components.description: A 2-dimensional data structure where each row is an item and each column is a data point.

props.name: cellPadding
props.description:
Specify the amount of space that should be available around the contents of
a cell

props.name: data
props.description: Provide a collection of the rows which will be rendered inside of the table

props.name: columns
props.description:
Provide the columns for the table and the fields in `data` to which they
correspond

props.name: initialSortColumn
props.description:
Provide the id or field of the column by which the table is sorted. When
using this `prop`, the input data must be sorted by this column in
ascending order

props.name: initialSortDirection
props.description:
Provide the sort direction that the table should be sorted by on the
currently sorted column

props.name: externalSorting
props.description:
When true, disables client-side sorting for all sortable columns in the
table. Use this when sorting is handled server-side. The `onToggleSort`
callback will still be fired when a sortable column header is clicked.

props.name: getRowId
props.description:
Provide a function to determine the unique identifier for each row.
This function allows you to customize the key used for the row.
By default, the table uses the `id` field from the data.

props.name: variant
props.description:
Secondary text style variations.

- `"inline"` - Secondary text is positioned beside primary text.
- `"block"` - Secondary text is positioned below primary text.

props.name: truncate
props.description: Whether the inline description should truncate the text on overflow.

components.name: Details
components.description: A styled component to enhance the native <details> element.

components.name: Dialog
components.description:
Props that characterize a button to be rendered into the footer of
a Dialog.

props.name: title
props.type: string | number | bigint | false | true | ReactElement<unknown, string | JSXElementConstructor<any>> | Iterable<ReactNode> | ReactPortal | Promise<AwaitedReactNode>
props.description: Title of the Dialog. Also serves as the aria-label for this Dialog.

props.name: subtitle
props.type: string | number | bigint | false | true | ReactElement<unknown, string | JSXElementConstructor<any>> | Iterable<ReactNode> | ReactPortal | Promise<AwaitedReactNode>
props.description:
The Dialog's subtitle. Optional. Rendered below the title in smaller
type with less contrast. Also serves as the aria-describedby for this
Dialog.

props.name: renderHeader
props.description:
Provide a custom renderer for the dialog header. This content is
rendered directly into the dialog body area, full bleed from edge
to edge, top to the start of the body element.

Warning: using a custom renderer may violate Primer UX principles.

props.name: renderBody
props.description:
Provide a custom render function for the dialog body. This content is
rendered directly into the dialog body area, full bleed from edge to
edge, header to footer.

Warning: using a custom renderer may violate Primer UX principles.

props.name: renderFooter
props.description:
Provide a custom render function for the dialog footer. This content is
rendered directly into the dialog footer area, full bleed from edge to
edge, end of the body element to bottom.

Warning: using a custom renderer may violate Primer UX principles.

props.name: footerButtons
props.description: Specifies the buttons to be rendered in the Dialog footer.

props.name: role
props.description: Default: "dialog". The ARIA role to assign to this dialog.

props.name: width
props.description:
The width of the dialog.
small: 296px
medium: 320px
large: 480px
xlarge: 640px

props.name: height
props.description:
The height of the dialog.
small: 296x480
large: 480x640
auto: variable based on contents

props.name: position
props.description: The position of the dialog

props.name: align
props.description:
The vertical alignment of the dialog. Only applies when position is 'center' (the default).
top: positions the Dialog ~4rem from the top of the screen, horizontally centered
center: (default) vertically centers the Dialog on the screen
bottom: positions the Dialog near the bottom of the screen, horizontally centered

props.name: returnFocusRef
props.description:
Return focus to this element when the Dialog closes,
instead of the element that had focus immediately before the Dialog opened

props.name: initialFocusRef
props.description: The element to focus when the Dialog opens

props.name: className
props.description: Additional class names to apply to the dialog

props.name: style
props.description: Additional styles to apply to the dialog

props.name: children
props.type: string | number | bigint | false | true | ReactElement<unknown, string | JSXElementConstructor<any>> | Iterable<ReactNode> | ReactPortal | Promise<AwaitedReactNode>

components.name: Divider
components.description: Visually separates `Items` or `Groups` in an `ActionList`.

props.name: title
props.description: Provide an optional title for the dialog

components.name: FilteredActionList
components.description: An ActionList with built-in filtering.

props.name: scrollContainerRef
props.description: A ref assigned to the scrollable container wrapping the ActionList

props.name: message
props.type: string | number | bigint | false | true | ReactElement<unknown, string | JSXElementConstructor<any>> | Iterable<ReactNode> | ReactPortal | Promise<AwaitedReactNode>

props.name: actionListProps
props.description: Additional props to pass to the underlying ActionList component.

props.name: focusOutBehavior
props.description:
Determines how keyboard focus behaves when navigating beyond the first or last item in the list.

- `'stop'`: Focus will stop at the first or last item; further navigation in that direction will not move focus.
- `'wrap'`: Focus will wrap around to the opposite end of the list when navigating past the boundaries (e.g., pressing Down on the last item moves focus to the first).

props.name: disableSelectOnHover
props.description: If true, disables selecting items when hovering over them with the mouse.

props.name: setInitialFocus
props.description:
If true, focus remains where it was and the user must interact to move focus.
If false, sets initial focus to the first item in the list when rendered, enabling keyboard navigation immediately.

props.name: focusPrependedElements
props.description:
Set to true to allow focus to move to elements that are dynamically prepended to the container.
Default is false.

props.name: scrollBehavior
props.description: Determines the scroll behavior of the container when an item is focused.

props.name: virtualized
props.description:
If true, enables client-side list virtualization. Only the visible items (plus a small
overscan buffer) are rendered in the DOM, dramatically improving performance for large lists.

This is a purely client-side optimization — it does not require server-side pagination.
The consumer can still pass all items at once; the component will only render what is visible.

Recommended for lists with more than 100 items.

Note: Has no effect when `groupMetadata` is provided, as grouped lists are
typically small enough not to need virtualization.

props.name: groupMetadata
props.description:
A collection of `Group` props (except `items`), plus a unique group identifier
and `Group`-level custom `Item` or `Group` renderers.

components.name: Flash
components.description: Used to inform users of successful or pending actions.

components.name: FormControl
components.description: Displays a labelled input and optionally associated validation and hint text.

props.name: children
props.type: string | number | bigint | false | true | ReactElement<unknown, string | JSXElementConstructor<any>> | Iterable<ReactNode> | ReactPortal | Promise<AwaitedReactNode>

props.name: disabled
props.description: Whether the control allows user input

props.name: id
props.description: The unique identifier for this control. Used to associate the label, validation text, and caption text

props.name: required
props.description: If true, the user must specify a value for the input before the owning form can be submitted

props.name: layout
props.description:
The direction the content flows.
Vertical layout is used by default, and horizontal layout is used for checkbox and radio inputs.

props.name: variant
props.description:
Style variations. Usage is discretionary.

- `"filled"` - Superimposed on a background, offset from nearby content
- `"subtle"` - Relatively less offset from nearby content

props.name: auxiliaryText
props.description: Secondary text which provides additional information about a `Group`.

props.name: selectionVariant
props.description: Whether multiple Items or a single Item can be selected in the Group. Overrides value on ActionList root.

components.name: GroupHeading
components.description:
Heading of  a `Group`.

As default, the role of ActionList is "list" and therefore group heading is rendered as a proper heading tag.
If the role is "listbox" or "menu" (ActionMenu), the group heading is rendered as a div with presentation role and it is
hidden from the accessibility tree due to the limitation of listbox children. https://w3c.github.io/aria/#listbox
groups under menu or listbox are labelled by `aria-label`

props.name: variant
props.description:
Style variations. Usage is discretionary.

- `"filled"` - Superimposed on a background, offset from nearby content
- `"subtle"` - Relatively less offset from nearby content

props.name: auxiliaryText
props.description: Secondary text which provides additional information about a `Group`.

props.description: A custom leading visual (icon or other element) to display instead of the default variant icon.

props.name: handleAddItem
props.type: (item: FC<PropsWithChildren<{ onClick?: (event: MouseEvent<HTMLElement, MouseEvent>) => void; onKeyPress?: (event: KeyboardEvent<HTMLElement>) => void; ... 5 more ...; className?: string; }>>) => void

components.name: KeybindingHint
components.description: Indicates the presence of an available keybinding.

props.name: keys
props.description:
The keys involved in this keybinding. These should be the full names of the keys as would
be returned by `KeyboardEvent.key` (e.g. "Control", "Shift", "ArrowUp", "a", etc.).

Combine keys with the "+" character to form chords. To represent the "+" key, use "Plus".

Combine chords/keys with " " to form sequences that should be pressed one after the other. For example, "a b"
represents "a then b". To represent the " " key, use "Space".

The fake key name "Mod" can be used to represent "Command" on macOS and "Control" on other platforms.

See https://github.com/github/hotkey for format details.

props.name: format
props.description:
Control the display format. Condensed is most useful in menus and tooltips, while
the full form is better for prose.

props.name: variant
props.description: Set to `onEmphasis` for display on emphasis colors, and `onPrimary` for display on primary buttons.

props.name: size
props.description: Control the size of the hint.

props.name: className
props.description: Additional class name to apply to the hint.

components.name: Label
components.description: Used to add contextual metadata to a design.

components.name: LabelGroup
components.description: Layout constraints for groups of Labels.

props.name: as
props.description: Customize the element type of the rendered container

props.name: overflowStyle
props.description: How hidden tokens should be shown. `'inline'` shows the hidden tokens after the visible tokens. `'overlay'` shows all tokens in an overlay that appears on top of the visible tokens.

props.name: visibleChildCount
props.description: How many tokens to show. `'auto'` truncates the tokens to fit in the parent container. Passing a number will truncate after that number tokens. If this is undefined, tokens will never be truncated.

components.name: Link
components.description: Used to apply styles to hyperlink text.

components.name: NavList
components.description: Renders a vertical list of navigation links.

props.name: children
props.type: string | number | bigint | false | true | ReactElement<unknown, string | JSXElementConstructor<any>> | Iterable<ReactNode> | ReactPortal | Promise<AwaitedReactNode>

components.name: Overlay
components.description: Codifies design patterns related to floating surfaces such as Dialogs and menus.

components.name: PageHeader
components.description: Determines the top-level headings of a UI.

components.name: PageLayout
components.description: Defines the header, main, pane, and footer areas of a page.

props.name: containerWidth
props.description: The maximum width of the page container

props.name: padding
props.description: The spacing between the outer edges of the page container and the viewport

components.name: Pagination
components.description: A horizontal set of links to navigate paginated content.

props.name: width
props.description:
The width of the pane.
- Named sizes: `'small'` | `'medium'` | `'large'`
- Custom object: `{min: string, default: string, max: string}`

When `resizable` is enabled, this defines the default width and constraints
(min/max bounds for dragging). Use `currentWidth` to control the displayed width.

props.name: minWidth
props.description:
Minimum width of the pane in pixels. Only used with named `width` sizes.
Ignored when `width` is a custom object (use `width.min` instead).

props.name: widthStorageKey
props.description:
localStorage key used to persist the pane width across sessions.
Only applies when `resizable` is `true` and no `onResizeEnd` callback is provided.

props.name: resizable
props.description:
Enable resizable pane behavior.
When `true`, the pane may be resized by the user via drag or keyboard.
Uses localStorage persistence by default unless `onResizeEnd` is provided.

Note: With default localStorage persistence in SSR, the server-rendered
width may differ from the stored client width, causing a brief layout
shift on hydration. Use `onResizeEnd` with server-aware storage to avoid this.

props.name: currentWidth
props.description:
Current/controlled width value in pixels.
When provided, this is used as the current pane width instead of internal state.
The `width` prop still defines the default used when resetting (e.g., double-click).
Pass `undefined` when the persisted value has not loaded yet (e.g., async fetch).

components.name: Popover
components.description: Used to bring attention to specific user interface elements.

props.name: className
props.description: Class name for custom styling

components.name: ProgressBar
components.description: A simple chart to show how complete something is, or visualize parts of a whole.

components.name: Radio
components.description: A form control for making a single selection from a short list of options.

props.name: value
props.description:
A unique value that is never shown to the user.
Used during form submission and to identify which radio button in a group is selected

props.name: name
props.description: Name attribute of the input element. Required for grouping radio inputs

props.name: disabled
props.description: Apply inactive visual appearance to the radio button

props.name: checked
props.description: Indicates whether the radio button is selected

props.name: required
props.description: Indicates whether the radio button must be checked before the form can be submitted

components.name: RadioGroup
components.description: Used to render a short list of mutually exclusive options.

props.name: name
props.description: The name used to identify this group of radios

props.name: className
props.description: Class name for custom styling

props.name: disabled
props.description: Whether the input group allows user input

props.name: id
props.description:
The unique identifier for this input group. Used to associate the label, validation text, and caption text.
You may want a custom ID to make it easier to select elements in integration tests.

props.name: required
props.description: If true, the user must make a selection before the owning form can be submitted

components.name: RelativeTime
components.description: Displays time in a way that is clear, concise, and accessible.

props.name: text
props.description: Primary text which names an `Item`.

props.name: description
props.description: Secondary text which provides additional information about an `Item`.

props.name: descriptionVariant
props.description:
Secondary text style variations. Usage is discretionary.

- `"inline"` - Secondary text is positioned beside primary text.
- `"block"` - Secondary text is positioned below primary text.

props.name: leadingVisual
props.description: Icon (or similar) positioned before `Item` text.

props.name: trailingVisual
props.type: string | number | bigint | false | true | ReactElement<unknown, string | JSXElementConstructor<any>> | Iterable<ReactNode> | ReactPortal | Promise<AwaitedReactNode> | ComponentClass<any, any>
props.description: Icon or text positioned after `Item` text.

props.name: variant
props.description:
Style variations associated with various `Item` types.

- `"default"` - An action `Item`.
- `"danger"` - A destructive action `Item`.

props.name: selected
props.description: For `Item`s which can be selected, whether the `Item` is currently selected.

props.name: groupId
props.description: Designates the group that an item belongs to.

props.name: disabled
props.description: Items that are disabled can not be clicked, selected, or navigated through.

props.name: id
props.description: An id associated with this item.  Should be unique between items

props.name: children
props.type: string | number | bigint | false | true | ReactElement<unknown, string | JSXElementConstructor<any>> | Iterable<ReactNode> | ReactPortal | Promise<AwaitedReactNode>
props.description: Node to be included inside the item before the text.

props.name: role
props.description: The ARIA role describing the function of `List` component. `option` is a common value.

props.name: className
props.description: className to apply to the list item

components.name: ScrollableRegion
components.description: A scrollable container with accessible keyboard scrolling.

components.name: SegmentedControl
components.description: Used to pick one choice from a linear set of closely related choices.

props.name: fullWidth
props.description: Whether the control fills the width of its parent

props.name: size
props.description: The size of the buttons

props.name: variant
props.description: Configure alternative ways to render the control when it gets rendered in tight spaces

props.name: icon
props.description: The icon that represents the segmented control item

props.name: selected
props.description: Whether the segment is selected. This is used for controlled SegmentedControls, and needs to be updated using the onChange handler on SegmentedControl.

props.name: defaultSelected
props.description: Whether the segment is selected. This is used for uncontrolled SegmentedControls to pick one SegmentedControlButton that is selected on the initial render.

props.name: description
props.description: Supplementary description that renders inside tooltip in place of the label.

props.name: tooltipDirection
props.description: The direction for the tooltip.

props.name: disabled
props.description: Whether the button is disabled.

components.name: Select
components.description: For choosing a single option from a dropdown menu of predefined choices.

props.name: selected
props.description: Is the `Item` is currently selected?

components.name: SelectPanel
components.description: An anchored dialog for quickly navigating and selecting from a list.

props.name: overlayProps
props.type: Partial<Omit<DetailedHTMLProps<HTMLAttributes<HTMLDivElement>, HTMLDivElement>, "height" | "width" | "style" | ... 5 more ... | keyof ContainerProps> & Omit<...> & ContainerProps & { ...; }>

props.name: notice
props.type: { text: string | ReactElement<any, string | JSXElementConstructor<any>>; variant: "error" | "info" | "warning"; }

props.name: message
props.type: { title: string; body: string | ReactElement<any, string | JSXElementConstructor<any>>; variant: "error" | "warning" | "empty"; icon?: ComponentType<...>; action?: ReactElement<...>; }

props.name: disableFullscreenOnNarrow
props.description:
Whether to disable fullscreen behavior on narrow viewports.
When `true`, the panel will maintain its anchored position regardless of viewport size.
When `false`, the panel will go fullscreen on narrow viewports (if feature flag is enabled).

props.name: focusPrependedElements
props.description:
Set to true to allow focus to move to elements that are dynamically prepended to the container.
Default is false.

props.name: focusOutBehavior
props.description:
Determines how keyboard focus behaves when navigating beyond the first or last item in the list.

- `'stop'`: Focus will stop at the first or last item; further navigation in that direction will not move focus.
- `'wrap'`: Focus will wrap around to the opposite end of the list when navigating past the boundaries (e.g., pressing Down on the last item moves focus to the first).

props.name: id
props.description: id to attach to the base DOM node of the list

props.name: role
props.description: The ARIA role describing the function of `List` component. `listbox` is a common value.

props.name: items
props.description: A collection of `Item` props and `Item`-level custom `Item` renderers.

props.name: renderItem
props.description:
A `List`-level custom `Item` renderer. Every `Item` within this `List`
without a `Group`-level or `Item`-level custom `Item` renderer will be
rendered using this function component.

props.name: children
props.type: string | number | bigint | false | true | ReactElement<unknown, string | JSXElementConstructor<any>> | Iterable<ReactNode> | ReactPortal | Promise<AwaitedReactNode>

props.name: icon
props.description:
Custom icon to display above the title.
When not provided, uses AlertIcon for error/warning states and no icon for empty state.

props.name: action
props.description:
Custom action element to display below the message body.
This can be used to render interactive elements like buttons or links.
Ensure the action element is accessible by providing appropriate ARIA attributes
and making it keyboard-navigable.

props.name: position
props.description: Position of the sidebar relative to the page layout

props.name: width
props.description: Width configuration for the sidebar

props.name: minWidth
props.description: Minimum width of the sidebar when resizable

props.name: resizable
props.description: Whether the sidebar can be resized

props.name: widthStorageKey
props.description:
localStorage key used to persist the sidebar width across sessions.
Only applies when `resizable` is `true`.
When omitted, localStorage is not used.

props.name: padding
props.description: Padding inside the sidebar

props.name: divider
props.description: Divider style between sidebar and content

props.name: sticky
props.description:
Whether the sidebar sticks to the viewport when scrolling.
When enabled, the sidebar uses `position: sticky` with `top: 0` and `height: 100vh`.

props.name: responsiveVariant
props.description:
Controls sidebar behavior at narrow viewport widths (below 768px).
- `'default'`: the sidebar retains its normal inline layout.
- `'fullscreen'`: the sidebar expands to cover the full viewport like a dialog overlay.

props.name: hidden
props.description: Whether the sidebar is hidden

props.name: id
props.description: Optional id for the sidebar element

props.name: children
props.type: string | number | bigint | false | true | ReactElement<unknown, string | JSXElementConstructor<any>> | Iterable<ReactNode> | ReactPortal | Promise<AwaitedReactNode>

components.name: SkeletonAvatar
components.description: Placeholder for loading Avatars.

props.name: className
props.description: Class name for custom styling

components.name: SkeletonBox
components.description: Placeholder for non-text, non-Avatar loading elements.

props.name: height
props.description: Height of the skeleton "box". Accepts any valid CSS `height` value.

props.name: width
props.description: Width of the skeleton "box". Accepts any valid CSS `width` value.

props.name: className
props.description: The className of the skeleton box

props.name: delay
props.description: Controls whether and how long to delay rendering the SkeletonBox. Set to 'short' to delay by 300ms, 'long' to delay by 1000ms, or provide a custom number of milliseconds.

components.name: SkeletonText
components.description: Placeholder for loading text.

props.name: size
props.description: Size of the text that the skeleton is replacing.

props.name: lines
props.description: Number of lines of skeleton text to render.

props.name: maxWidth
props.description: Maximum width that the line(s) of skeleton text can take up.  Accepts any valid CSS `max-width` value.

props.name: className
props.description: Class name for custom styling

components.name: Spinner
components.description: An indeterminate loading indicator.

props.name: size
props.description: Sets the width and height of the spinner.

props.name: srText
props.description: Sets the text conveyed by assistive technologies such as screen readers. Set to `null` if the loading state is displayed in a text node somewhere else on the page.

props.name: delay
props.description: Controls whether and how long to delay rendering the spinner. Set to `true` to delay by 1000ms, `'short'` to delay by 300ms, `'long'` to delay by 1000ms, or provide a custom number of milliseconds.

components.name: SplitPageLayout
components.description: A layout with a two-column layout: main content area and sidebar.

components.name: Stack
components.description: A layout component that creates responsive horizontal and vertical flows.

props.name: as
props.description: Customize the element type of the rendered container

props.name: as
props.description: Customize the element type of the rendered container

components.name: StateLabel
components.description: Used for rendering the status of an issue or pull request.

props.type: string | number | bigint | false | true | ReactElement<unknown, string | JSXElementConstructor<any>> | Iterable<ReactNode> | ReactPortal | Promise<AwaitedReactNode>

props.type: string | number | bigint | false | true | ReactElement<unknown, string | JSXElementConstructor<any>> | Iterable<ReactNode> | ReactPortal | Promise<AwaitedReactNode>

props.name: as
props.description: HTML element to render summary as.

props.name: children
props.type: string | number | bigint | false | true | ReactElement<unknown, string | JSXElementConstructor<any>> | Iterable<ReactNode> | ReactPortal | Promise<AwaitedReactNode>

components.name: Table
components.description: A composable HTML table with Primer styling for data display.

props.name: align
props.description: The horizontal alignment of the cell's content

props.name: align
props.description: The horizontal alignment of the cell's content

props.name: columns
props.description:
Provide an array of columns for the table. Columns will render as the headers
of the table.

props.name: rows
props.description:
Optionally specify the number of rows which should be included in the
skeleton state of the component

props.name: align
props.description: The horizontal alignment of the cell's content

props.name: direction
props.description: Specify the sort direction for the TableHeader

props.name: as
props.description:
Provide an alternate element or component to use as the container for
`TableSubtitle`. This is useful when specifying markup that is more
semantic for your use-case

props.name: id
props.description:
Provide a unique id for the table subtitle. This should be used along with
`aria-describedby` on `DataTable`

props.name: id
props.description:
Provide a unique id for the table subtitle. This should be used along with
`aria-labelledby` on `DataTable`

props.name: as
props.description:
Provide an alternate element or component to use as the container for
`TableSubtitle`. This is useful when specifying markup that is more
semantic for your use-case, such as a heading tag.

props.name: value
props.description:
Specify the selected tab
`value` can only be used in the controlled variant of the component
If you need to use `value`, please switch to the controlled variant by removing the `defaultValue` prop.

props.name: defaultValue
props.description:
`defaultValue` can only be used in the uncontrolled variant of the component
If you need to use `defaultValue`, please switch to the uncontrolled variant by removing the `value` prop.
Specify the default selected tab

components.name: Text
components.description: An abstraction for using Primer's typographic styles.

components.name: Textarea
components.description: Used to set a value that is multiple lines of text.

props.name: disabled
props.description: Apply inactive visual appearance to the Textarea

props.name: validationStatus
props.description: Indicates whether the Textarea validation state

props.name: block
props.description: Block

props.name: resize
props.description: Allows resizing of the textarea

props.name: contrast
props.description: apply a high contrast color to background

props.name: className
props.description: The className to apply to the wrapper element

props.name: minHeight
props.description: The minimum height of the Textarea

props.name: maxHeight
props.description: The maximum height of the Textarea

props.name: style
props.description: CSS styles to apply to the Textarea

props.name: characterLimit
props.description:
Optional character limit for the textarea. If provided, a character counter will be displayed below the textarea.
When the limit is exceeded, validation styling will be applied.

components.name: TextInput
components.description: Used to set a value that is a single line of text.

components.name: TextInputWithTokens
components.description: An input for a value that is a list.

props.name: tokenComponent
props.description: The component used to render each token

props.name: maxHeight
props.description:
The maximum height of the component. If the content in the input exceeds this height,
it will scroll vertically

props.name: preventTokenWrapping
props.description: Whether tokens should render inline horizontally. By default, tokens wrap to new lines.

props.name: size
props.description: The size of the tokens and text input

props.name: hideTokenRemoveButtons
props.description: Whether the remove buttons should be rendered in the tokens

props.name: visibleTokenCount
props.description: The number of tokens to display before truncating

props.name: loading
props.description: Whether the to show a loading indicator in the input

props.name: leadingVisual
props.type: string | number | bigint | false | true | ReactElement<unknown, string | JSXElementConstructor<any>> | Iterable<ReactNode> | ReactPortal | Promise<AwaitedReactNode> | ComponentClass<any, any>
props.description: A visual that renders inside the input before the typing area

props.name: trailingVisual
props.type: string | number | bigint | false | true | ReactElement<unknown, string | JSXElementConstructor<any>> | Iterable<ReactNode> | ReactPortal | Promise<AwaitedReactNode> | ComponentClass<any, any>
props.description: A visual that renders inside the input after the typing area

props.name: trailingAction
props.description: A visual that renders inside the input after the typing area

components.name: Timeline
components.description: Used to display items on a vertical Timeline.

components.name: ToggleSwitch
components.description: Used to immediately toggle a setting on or off.

props.name: defaultChecked
props.description: Uncontrolled - whether the switch is turned on

props.name: disabled
props.description: Whether the switch is ready for user input

props.name: loading
props.description: Whether the switch's value is being calculated

props.name: checked
props.description: Whether the switch is turned on

props.name: size
props.description: Size of the switch

props.name: statusLabelPosition
props.description:
Whether the "on" and "off" labels should appear before or after the switch.
**This should only be changed when the switch's alignment needs to be adjusted.** For example: It needs to be left-aligned because the label appears above it and the caption appears below it.

props.name: loadingLabelDelay
props.description:
If the switch is in the loading state, this value controls the amount of delay in milliseconds before
the `loadingLabel` is announced to screen readers.

props.name: loadingLabel
props.description:
The text to describe what is loading. It should be descriptive and not verbose.
This is primarily used for AT (screen readers) to convey what is currently loading.

props.name: buttonType
props.description: type of button to account for behavior when added to a form

props.name: buttonLabelOn
props.description: Text to display when the toggle switch is in the 'on' position. Defaults to 'On'. Only customize this label if there is a more specific label for the context. For example, you might use 'Show' if the setting is 'Show images'.

props.name: buttonLabelOff
props.description: Text to display when the toggle switch is in the 'off' position. Defaults to 'Off'. Only customize this label if there is a more specific label for the context. For example, you might use 'Hide' if the setting is 'Show images'.

components.name: Token
components.description: A compact representation of an object, typically used for related metadata.

components.name: Tooltip
components.description: Adds additional context to interactive UI elements on hover or keyboard focus.

props.name: delay
props.description: Delay in milliseconds before showing the tooltip

components.name: TopicTag
components.description: Used to display a topic tag.

props.name: as
props.description: The HTML element or React component to render as the root element

props.name: className
props.description: Provide a class name for styling on the outermost element

components.name: TreeView
components.description: A hierarchical list of items that can be expanded or collapsed.

components.name: Truncate
components.description: Used to shorten overflowing text with an ellipsis.

components.name: UnderlineNav
components.description: Used to display navigation in a horizontal tabbed format.

props.name: children
props.type: string | number | bigint | false | true | ReactElement<unknown, string | JSXElementConstructor<any>> | Iterable<ReactNode> | ReactPortal | Promise<AwaitedReactNode>

props.name: loadingCounters
props.description: loading state for all counters. It displays loading animation for individual counters (UnderlineNav.Item) until all are resolved. It is needed to prevent multiple layout shift.

props.name: variant
props.description:
There are cases where you may not want the horizontal padding on items,
and panels to make the tabs look horizontally aligned with the content above and below it.
Setting this to `flush` will remove the horizontal padding on the items.

props.name: as
props.description: Renders `UnderlineNav.Item` as given component i.e. react-router's Link

components.name: UnderlinePanels
components.description: Used to break related content into tabbed panels.

props.name: children
props.type: string | number | bigint | false | true | ReactElement<unknown, string | JSXElementConstructor<any>> | Iterable<ReactNode> | ReactPortal | Promise<AwaitedReactNode>
props.description: Tabs (UnderlinePanels.Tab) and panels (UnderlinePanels.Panel) to render

props.name: id
props.description: Custom string to use when generating the IDs of tabs and `aria-labelledby` for the panels

props.name: loadingCounters
props.description: Loading state for all counters. It displays loading animation for individual counters until all are resolved. It is needed to prevent multiple layout shift.

props.name: className
props.description: Class name for custom styling

props.name: as
props.description: Element type for the tab container

components.name: VisuallyHidden
components.description: Visually hides content while keeping it accessible to screen readers.
